meta.description: Designs primary, secondary, and exploratory endpoints for biomedical and clinical research protocols. Always use this skill when a user needs to translate study aims into operational endpoint definitions with event rules, assessment timing, composite logic, interpretability, and

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced.

methodological_ground.detail: Composite endpoint justification rule and surrogate-labeling hard rule are methodologically rigorous

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 2 endpoint design planning only

functional_suitability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

reliability.note: Mandatory clarification gate is excellent; one gap: provisional scaffold not always labeled as such when produced

performance_context.note: 280 lines with ten reference modules — well-balanced progressive disclosure

agent_usability.note: Full marks (16/16); no significant issues detected.

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

agent_specific.note: Three-tier final status label (provisional/workable/operational) is an excellent usability signal; description strong

inputs.label: Primary and secondary endpoints for retrospective septic shock cohort

assertions.text: Primary endpoint defined in clear operational terms with event triggers
Event definition and assessment timing specified in Section E
Operationalization table present mapping endpoints to capture sources
Surrogate endpoints labeled as not self-validating
Final draft status label (provisional/workable/operational) present

inputs.label: Prognostic biomarker endpoints in pancreatic cancer cohort

assertions.text: Biomarker endpoint separated from clinical outcome endpoint
Baseline variable not mixed with endpoint definition
Endpoint hierarchy with primary/secondary/exploratory separation present
Section H bias and interpretability review present
No fabricated event rates or validation performance

inputs.label: Composite endpoint design for immunotherapy response and survival

assertions.text: Composite endpoint justification explicitly stated with component rationale
First-event rule and heterogeneous severity handling addressed
Alternative to composite (separate endpoints) compared
Composite does not combine events merely to increase event count
Operational capture source specified per component

inputs.label: Vague request: 'design endpoints for our study' without disease or study type

assertions.text: Clarification-first rule applied; disease context and objective requested before long output
At most minimal provisional scaffold produced before clarification
Focused follow-up questions are high-yield and concise (≤5)
Provisional scaffold labeled as provisional if produced
assertions.note: Brief provisional scaffold produced without 'PROVISIONAL' label — could be mistaken for operational output

assertions.text: No endpoints invented from unspecified context

inputs.label: Complex multi-endpoint protocol with time-to-event, binary, and continuous outcomes

assertions.text: Fixed-horizon vs time-to-event structure not mixed without explicit labeling
Competing risk structure addressed when relevant
Endpoint hierarchy prevents exploratory overload
Weakest endpoint choice identified in self-critical review
No more than one true primary endpoint nominated

key_strengths: Mandatory clarification gate before long-form output is one of the strongest UX safeguards in this skill collection
Three-tier final status label (provisional/workable/operational) gives users clear deployment readiness signal
Operationalization table (Section G) is a unique and highly practical deliverable absent in most endpoint design tools
Twelve hard rules covering surrogate labeling, composite endpoint justification, and ascertainment bias are methodologically comprehensive

recommendations.title: Provisional scaffold not consistently labeled when produced before clarification
recommendations.problem: When the clarification gate triggers but the skill still produces a brief scaffold, that scaffold is not always marked as 'PROVISIONAL — requires clarification before use'.
recommendations.root_cause: The clarification-first rule says 'may produce a minimal provisional scaffold, clearly labeled as provisional' but the labeling instruction is not enforced consistently.
recommendations.fix: Add a hard rule: any output produced before clarification is complete must begin with a bold 'PROVISIONAL DRAFT — not suitable for protocol use until clarification questions are answered' header.

recommendations.title: No guidance on what makes a surrogate endpoint acceptable vs unacceptable
recommendations.problem: Hard Rule 6 says surrogates must be labeled as not self-validating, but the skill does not provide criteria for when a surrogate is acceptable vs should be replaced with a clinical endpoint.
recommendations.root_cause: The ambiguity-and-interpretability-rules.md reference handles this, but no summary guidance appears in SKILL.md itself.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief surrogate acceptability note to Hard Rule 6: state the minimum requirements for a defensible surrogate (e.g., strong biological plausibility + at least one external validation study linking the surrogate to a patient-meaningful outcome).
